description: N-1 pin (C-2 fix part b, the degenerate rule-2b guard). Two correction_detected observations at the SAME observed_at (T0) correct the same original 'sonet' to DIFFERENT values ('Sonnet' then 'Sonet'). vocab.corrects_to is single-valued with valid_from=observed_at, so the second candidate is a different-object, SAME-valid_from case: without the guard, rule 2b builds a zero-width [T0,T0) historical era, violating the schema CHECK (valid_to>valid_from) => IntegrityError => dead-lettered batch. Realistic in vocab-file imports that share a single file timestamp. The guard drops the later-arriving candidate + notes instead. Expected: NO crash; exactly ONE open vocab fact ('Sonnet', the first-arriving), both observations stored. fact_count 2 = 1 session.occurred + 1 vocab.corrects_to.
